source.title: EU countries agree temporary €3 flat customs fee for small imported parcels - Euronews

extractions.claim: EU €150 de minimis elimination: €3/parcel customs charge effective July 1, 2026. Additional €2 handling fee planned November 2026 (5 countries pushing for July 1 sync).
extractions.evidence: EU will introduce flat €3 ($3.50) per package customs charge on e-commerce parcels valued under €150, effective July 1, 2026. EU also plans to enact €2 customs handling fee in November 2026. France, Belgium, Germany, Luxembourg, and Netherlands pushing for July 1, 2026 adoption to synchronize with €3 fee. Interim until 2028 Customs Data Hub rollout.

verification.verifier_notes: Euronews and EC Taxation & Customs Union official notice (Nov 13, 2025)

extractions.claim: 4.6 billion parcels under €150 entered EU in 2024, 90%+ from China. 65% undervalued or split to stay under threshold. ~€1B annual revenue loss.
extractions.evidence: EC estimates approximately 4.6 billion parcels valued under €150 entered the EU in 2024, more than 90% from China. Over 65% were undervalued or split into multiple packages to stay under the €150 threshold, resulting in approximately €1 billion annual revenue loss.

verification.verifier_notes: European Commission estimates cited in multiple sources

extractions.claim: France anti-fast-fashion law: eco-tax starting €5/item in 2026, rising to €10 by 2030. Advertising ban on ultra-fast fashion from Jan 2026. Passed French Senate 337-1.
extractions.evidence: France passed legislation imposing eco-tax starting at €5 in 2026, rising to €10 by 2030, capped at 50% of retail price. Additional packaging tax of €2-€4 for light packages under 2kg from outside EU. Complete ban on advertising ultra-fast fashion starts January 2026. Approved by French Senate 337-1 on June 10, 2025.

verification.verifier_notes: Euronews reporting on French Senate vote. Still requires joint committee reconciliation and EC notification.
